tweets.text:
PHOTOS OF THE DAY: Saudi Arabia is loading simultaneously 4 supertankers in the Persian Gulf. Two started loading yesterday at the single-point moorings of Ju’aymah (see post below) with their AIS visible (and a 3rd is waiting nearby). This morning, two others appear to be loading at Ras Tanura's Sea Islands, the world's largest oil terminal. One is broadcasting on AIS, the other is dark.
📷 @CopernicusEU Sentinel-1 SAR 🛰️
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Saudi Arabia appears to be about re-starting oil loadings inside the Persian Gulf. 

Three Saudi-owned in ballast VLCCs are concentrating around the Ju’aymah terminal (it has 6 single point moorings).

If the loadings go ahead, it would be the first known since early March.

tweets.text:
What a truly lovely opportunity to remember, honor, and celebrate an intellectual giant in the world of finance.
A pioneer who consistently drove impactful and lasting innovations in fixed income and portfolio construction, Marty led with an inspiring grace and kindness that elevated everyone around him.
Our deepest thanks go out to his lovely family—his wife, Sarah, and his three daughters, Becky, Karen, and Kim—for sharing him with us and for hosting such a special event.
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text: Mostly the myth isn't whether it can be inflated away.  It can be.  Start by balancing the budget which is the really really hard thing which no cares to even try to accomplish.  Then have Nominal GDP grow at a rate higher than existing interest cost.   That slowly works over decades.  But it's a waste of time even discussing when deficits are 5-8% per year.
tweets.full_text: Mostly the myth isn't whether it can be inflated away.  It can be.  Start by balancing the budget which is the really really hard thing which no cares to even try to accomplish. tweets.text: Fwiw as a perma bear on $MSTR since 11/20/2024 I find it slightly interesting that I am in no way bearish the company based on any of the things that have caused me to be bearish to date.  This was a big change in price today.  I have no strong view on BTC and am not touching it until 42,069 so not buying the MSTR shares but I can no longer say one should buy BTC instead of the share.  In fact I would buy $30 of MSTR and $8 of STRC instead of buying $38 of BTC.  That said my view depends on @Saylor ceasing buying BTC and issuing stock and preferred to pay for it.  If he does I expect further discounting of the balance sheet.  At a more substantial discount of .8x I will probably buy this package and sell short BTC.  For now just punting long small on STRC and waiting for 42,069 on BTC

tweets.text:
Memory companies (like micron) are to memory what debeers is to diamonds.

“Supply constrained” because they understand that memory is a commodity + cyclical, and their way to milk it this time around without future overhang would be through limiting expansion, propping up margins.

Sounds like a conspiracy theory till you open their books for the last 8 quarters and compare the growth in cash flows with the lack of growth in expenditure to expand capacity.

tweets.text:
There's a global correction right now, no clue when it stops. 

Kospi is down -8.18% (Sk hynix/Samsung)
Nikkei is down -4.8%
TWSE Index is down -3.82%

As you've seen with $SOI or $RKLB dropping 30-40%. 

From personal experience, high beta stocks get hit a lot harder, but usually frontrun index drops. 

And they typically recover earlier? 

Aside from Korea which is typically volatile, when major indexes start correcting 3-4% a day, usually not a fun time.

tweets.text:
Looks like Power Semis are already starting price hikes.

Which is bullish thematically for US power semi trade from $AOSL to $POWI, before the 800V DC shift fully hits.

China's Yangjie Technology, announced a 2nd price increase this year, raising prices across its full product range by 10%-15%, July 1.

Chongqing China Resources Micro, Silan Microelectronics, and NCE Power also price hiked.

“Demand is playing a larger role than costs in the latest round of price increases” from AI DCs, to energy systems, and new EVs.

Another potential beneficiary is the upstream material companies that make these:

- nonferrous metals like copper and tin.
-Plastic molding compounds
- chemicals, and packaging consumables

“Nearly all major Chinese power semiconductor makers have raised prices in 2026.” (Digitimes)

So positive read through on power semi adjacent (reduce China-driven price pressure + better pricing + demand validation)

tweets.text:
andrew’s spot on. what do you think anthropic’s doing with all that spare fable compute right now?

they’re using it to build mythos 6 which will make current models look like toys.

the spice will continue to flow regardless of what the government bans and there’s only one loser in all of this:

you.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: For the people saying this is a pause, or a victory for safety, it is not. This does not slow development in any way, it only slows the rate at which the labs can 𝘳𝘦𝘭𝘦𝘢𝘴𝘦 models, not how fast they can train them. The gap between what is available to the public, and what

tweets.text:
worst fears confirmed. U.S. government Fable ban is now hitting openai’s upcoming gpt 5.6 model

access will be granted on a per-case basis (government decides)

this will have big implications down the road on access to frontier intelligence.

the days of universal model access is officially over.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
New w/ @leomschwartz @amir: 

The Trump admin has asked OpenAI to stagger the release of GPT-5.6 over security concerns. 

On Thursday, CEO Sam Altman told staff that the government will be approving access to GPT-5.6 customer by customer, a highly unusual approach. https://t.co/JEkGR97SAU
tweets.full_text:
worst fears confirmed. U.S. government Fable ban is now hitting openai’s upcoming gpt 5.6 model

access will be granted on a per-case basis (government decides)

this will have big implications down the road on access to frontier intelligence.

the days of universal model access is officially over.
